hook: Self-trust grows when your actions match the standards you say you have.
body: Confidence gets steadier when your nervous system can see that you do in fact protect what matters to you.

hook: Repeated self-betrayal weakens confidence more than rejection ever could.
body: Keeping your own standards is part of becoming someone you believe in.

hook: Keeping your own standards is part of becoming someone you believe in.
body: Every time you leave a connection that repeatedly violates your basics, your self-trust usually increases a little.

body: Pick one recurring place where you override yourself and stop doing it once on purpose.
